title: centaurea nigra var. endressii
intro: Centaurea nigra var. endressii, also known as Endress's knapweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Centaurea nigra var. endressii include its ability to soothe digestive problems, alleviate menstrual cramps, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas and infusions made from its leaves and flowers, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
